document_metadata.bylaw_name: Land Use By-law for Beaver Bank, Hammonds Plains and Upper Sackville

permitted_uses.use_name: Non-residential uses that existed on or before [coming in force date of this section]
Agricultural uses, except buildings and structures related to agricultural uses
Public and private parks and playgrounds
Boathouse accessory to an existing residential use
Change in use permitted uses Subject to section 25A.9, an existing non-residential use may change in use to a use permitted in the underlying zone except for any of the following:
Any use for the storage, distribution, or manufacturing of hazardous materials

provisions.text: FWO USES PERMITTED No development permit shall be issued in any FWO (Floodway Overlay) Zone except for the following:

FWO ZONE REQUIREMENTS: RESOURCE USES In any FWO Zone, where uses are permitted as Resource Uses, no development permit shall be issued except in conformity with the following: Minimum Lot Area 29,064 square feet (2700 m2) Minimum Frontage 100 feet (30.5 m) Minimum Front or Flankage Yard 20 feet (6.1 m) Minimum Rear or Side Yard 8 feet (2.4 m) Maximum Lot Coverage 35 percent

requirement_sections.title_label_raw: FWO ZONE REQUIREMENTS: OPEN SPACE USES

provisions.text: FWO ZONE REQUIREMENTS: OPEN SPACE USES In any FWO Zone, where uses are permitted as Open Space Uses, no development permit shall be issued except in conformity with the provisions of Sections 25A.4, 25A.5, 25A.6 of Part 25 and Sections 22.3 and 22.5 of Part 22.

requirement_sections.title_label_raw: OTHER REQUIREMENTS: PROHIBITED USES AND STRUCTURES

no structure shall be used for the storage, distribution, or manufacturing of

RESTRICTION: PLACEMENT OF OFF-SITE FILL The placement of off-site fill or alteration of grades is prohibited within the FWO Zone, except for road construction and public utilities. The placement of off-site fill or alteration of grades will only be permitted for road construction or public utilities where a professional engineer undertakes a hydrotechnical study to certify that the fill will not contribute to flooding on adjacent properties, increased upstream or downstream flooding, increased flood elevations, or result in a change to flood water flow patterns.

PARKING The minimum parking requirements, as set out in Section 4.26, shall not apply to any use permitted in the Floodway Overlay Zone.

content_blocks.text: No development permit shall be issued in any FWO (Floodway Overlay) Zone
In any FWO Zone, where uses are permitted as Resource Uses, no development

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: permit shall be issued except in conformity with the following:
content_blocks.text: Minimum Lot Area 29,064 square feet (2700 m2) Minimum Frontage 100 feet (30.5 m) Minimum Front or Flankage Yard 20 feet (6.1 m) Minimum Rear or Side Yard 8 feet (2.4 m) Maximum Lot Coverage 35 percent

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: 25A.3 FWO ZONE REQUIREMENTS: OPEN SPACE USES
content_blocks.text: In any FWO Zone, where uses are permitted as Open Space Uses, no development permit shall be issued except in conformity with the provisions of Sections 25A.4, 25A.5, 25A.6 of Part 25 and Sections 22.3 and 22.5 of Part 22.

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: 25A.4 OTHER REQUIREMENTS: PROHIBITED USES AND STRUCTURES

content_blocks.text: a) no structure shall be used or constructed to be used for human habitation, whether permanent or temporary, in any FWO Zone; b) no structure shall be used for the storage, distribution, or manufacturing of hazardous materials; and c) any structure must be designed by a professional engineer to withstand a flood and allow flow through.

The placement of off-site fill or alteration of grades is prohibited within the FWO Zone, except for road construction and public utilities. The placement of off-site fill or alteration of grades will only be permitted for road construction or public utilities where a professional engineer undertakes a hydrotechnical study to certify that the fill will not contribute to flooding on adjacent properties, increased upstream or downstream flooding, increased flood elevations, or result in a change to flood water flow patterns.

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: 25A.6 OTHER REQUIREMENTS: GRADE ALTERATION FOR FLOODPROOFING
content_blocks.text: Notwithstanding Section 25A.5, alteration of grades is permitted for

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: floodproofing required under Sections 25A.7 and 25A.8 subject to the following:
content_blocks.text: a) grade alteration is limited to the existing building footprint with associated slope stabilization to floodproof a building or structure. b) a grading plan has been prepared and certified by a professional engineer or licensed Nova Scotia Land Surveyor showing the final grade is above the nearest upstream 1-in-100-year flood elevation shown on Schedule K or at a site-specific elevation above the 1-in-100-year elevation as determined by a professional engineer or Nova Scotia Land Surveyor; c) a professional engineer, through a hydrotechnical study, certifies that the grading will not contribute to flooding on adjacent properties, increased upstream or downstream flooding, increased flood elevations, or result in a change to flood water flow patterns; and d) all other grading legislation is met.

Notwithstanding Sections 4.9 and 25A.1, where a hydrotechnical study undertaken by a professional engineer certifies that an expansion to an existing use will not contribute to flooding on adjacent properties, increased upstream or downstream flooding, increased flood elevations, result in a change to flood water flow patterns, existing uses permitted under Section 25A.1 are permitted to

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: expand provided the expansion is for:
content_blocks.text: a) alterations and expansions to entranceways needed for accessibility purposes; or b) alterations and expansions to loading areas.

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: 25A.8 OTHER REQUIREMENTS: EXISTING USE RECONSTRUCTION AND

content_blocks.text: Existing uses permitted under Section 25A.1, are permitted to be reconstructed provided the structure is constructed in the same location and to the same extent as the existing building, there is no increase in the building footprint, and a hydrotechnical study, carried out by a qualified person, shows that the proposed development will not contribute to increased flooding on adjacent properties, increased upstream or downstream flooding, increased flood elevations, or result in a change to floodwater flow patterns. Where an existing use is permitted to be

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: reconstructed the following floodproofing conditions must be met:
content_blocks.text: a) all floors must be constructed above the nearest upstream 1-in-100-year flood elevation outlined on Schedule K or above the site-specific 1-in-100-year elevation as determined by a professional engineer or Nova Scotia land surveyor. b) all electrical and mechanical equipment within a building must be located above the 1-in-100-year elevation unless designed by a qualified professional engineer to withstand a flood; and c) any structure constructed below the 1-in-100-year flood elevation must be designed by a professional engineer to withstand a flood and allow for flow through.

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: 25A.9 OTHER REQUIREMENTS: EXISTING NON-RESIDENTIAL USES CHANGE

content_blocks.text: An existing non-residential use may change to another non-residential use

content_blocks.heading_context_raw: permitted in the underlying zone subject to the following requirements:
content_blocks.text: a) there is no expansion of the structure(s); b) the change in use is not for any residential institutions such as hospitals, shared accommodations, or daycares as outlined in Section 25A.1; and c) the use change is not for commercial or industrial uses involving the use or storage of hazardous materials.
The minimum parking requirements, as set out in Section 4.26, shall not apply to any use permitted in the Floodway Overlay Zone.

open_issues.description: Clause patterns listed in pending_review_clause_patterns were preserved raw because their hierarchy normalization is not yet approved in this repository context.
